source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 10. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 230215 (Rev.5) - Label Update/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-2 Clinical Evaluation Report/Clinical Evaluation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-060, rev.1).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for the ADD digital flat panel X-ray detector supporting CE MDD conformity, safety, performance, benefit-risk, equivalence, literature, PMS and PMCF rationale.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co.,Ltd. as part of clinical evaluation and conformity evidence.
keyPoints: ADD is described as a Class IIa digital flat panel X-ray detector under Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The intended use is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, replacing film-based radiographic diagnostic systems, and not for mammography.
Clinical evaluation is based on literature review, equivalent predicate devices, PMS information, risk management, and MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4.
The report concludes that risks associated with use are acceptable when weighed against patient benefits.
The next clinical evaluation period is stated as 2022.12.01 to 2024.12.01.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation under MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4
concepts.evidence: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.

concepts.name: MDD Class IIa classification
concepts.evidence: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

concepts.name: Intended use for general radiographic diagnosis
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Not for mammography
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Equivalence demonstration
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance and PMCF
concepts.evidence: Through continuous PMS activity, the influence of the clinical performance and safety related to the device is constantly grasped in various aspects. In addition, PMCF studies will be conducted in accordance with the PMCF Plan if necessary.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk acceptability
concepts.evidence: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

evidence.claim: The document is a clinical evaluation report for ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision stated in the document body is A-TCF-5-060 Rev.2.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2)

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed and approved on 2022.12.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by ... Date 2022.12.01 ... Reviewed by ... Date 2022.12.01 ... Approved by ... Date 2022.12.01

evidence.claim: Revision 2 updated PMS and sales record sections.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 Revision following ‘PMS’ and ‘2.10 Sales record’ update. 2022.12.01

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co.,Ltd. is the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd.

evidence.claim: ADD is classified as Class IIa under MDD Annex IX Rule 16.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The target patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Target Patient Population A Adult and children except infant and pregnant women

evidence.claim: Contraindications include mammography, direct cardiac application, infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: ADD uses amorphous silicon thin-film technology with TFT and CsI scintillators.
evidence.evidenceText: The flat panel sensors of the ADD are fabricated using thin film technology based on amorphous silicon technology ... Each pixel in the array consists of a light‐sensing photodiode and a switching Thin Film Transistor (TFT) ... well matched to scintillators such as CsI.

evidence.claim: ADD supports wired or wireless digital flat panel detector operation and auto-trigger signal sensing.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors ... The detectors support an auto-trigger signal sensing technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation considered literature review, equivalent predicate device clinical data and post-market surveillance data according to MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation includes the overall results of literature review, extensive clinical data for substantially equivalent predicate device and post market surveillance data in accordance with MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4.

evidence.claim: The evaluation is part of the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, this clinical evaluation is part on the risk management process according to EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: The report lists MDD 93/42/EEC, RED 2014/53/EU, standards and MEDDEV guidance as relevant documents.
evidence.evidenceText: In the course of this clinical evaluation ... the following standards and guidelines are also taken into consideration: Council Directive 93/42/EEC ... Radio Equipment Directive 2014/53/EU ... MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.4 Clinical Evaluation

evidence.claim: ADD safety was evaluated against IEC 60601-1 limits for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD has been tested and found to comply with the limits for medical devices in IEC 60601-1.

evidence.claim: The device under evaluation was assessed using currently available scientific literature on equivalent products, not clinical investigations.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on scientific literature currently available A Clinical evaluations were performed using the currently available scientific literatures on equivalent products. Based on Clinical investigations made N/A

The report concludes the device can be expected to exhibit claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Considering the literature review, safety & performance test and comparison to similar devices, we concluded that this device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the risks associated with device use are acceptable when weighed against benefits to the patient.
evidence.evidenceText: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

evidence.claim: The next clinical evaluation period is stated as 2022.12.01 to 2024.12.01.
evidence.evidenceText: The next clinical evaluation 2022.12.01 ~ 2024.12.01 CE Surveillance audit

reviewFlags.reason: Revision metadata conflicts: sourcePath names the file as rev.1, while the document body states Report No.: A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2) and revision history includes Rev.2 dated 2022.12.01.

Authority is ambiguous: authoritySignal is general, while the document references competent authorities, notified bodies, EU MDD, RED, South Korea approval, EU certificate and U.S.A. K213497 without identifying a single governing authority for the report.

Sales evidence is internally inconsistent: the sales record table lists 1 domestic sale in 2021 and 8 overseas sales in 2022, while later PMS sections state there is no sales record during the period.
Low text quality/OCR or source conversion artifacts are present, including duplicated sections, typographical errors, incomplete sentence fragments such as 'The', and inconsistent spellings.
The document states the declaration of interests is held by the manufacturer and refers to Doc. No. A-TCF-5-065, but the detailed declaration document is not included in the provided source text.
